raw_response:
The correct answer would require information about the ideal depth for Mineral Trioxide Aggregate (MTA) in root-end fillings. However, none of the given references contain any direct information regarding the depth of MTA usage in dental applications. Therefore, we need to rely on standard guidelines typically found in dentistry literature:
Standard clinical recommendations suggest placing MTA up to 3mm below the apical level for effective sealing and prevention of microleakage while minimizing irritation to periapical tissues.
Thus, my conclusion aligns best with Option B:
\boxed{B} Based on typical clinical practices and standards, the ideal depth for MTA in root-end fillings is approximately 3 mm. Thus, I choose \boxed{B}.
